docs.title: Four-concept model — CTX, ADR, Task, Index
docs.summary: DocOps needs a minimal vocabulary that maps cleanly to how software gets built with LLMs. Existing tools fall into two traps: - Blurring task / decision / requirement boundaries (Backlog.md, Markplane…

docs.title: Bare-minimum frontmatter philosophy
docs.summary: Over-specified frontmatter rots fast: fields go unfilled, authors skip the doc because the ceremony feels heavy, and LLMs waste context on fields that are rarely queried. Under-specified frontmatter f…

docs.title: Filename is the canonical document ID
docs.summary: Many doc systems carry an `id:` field in frontmatter separate from the filename. This creates two sources of truth and a rename-drift problem: the file moves, the ID stays, references break or are con…

docs.title: Tasks must cite ≥1 ADR or CTX — the alignment contract
docs.summary: Existing task systems (Jira, Linear, Backlog.md, GSD plans, BMAD stories) permit tasks to be created without a structural link to the decision or stakeholder intent they advance. In practice this mean…

docs.title: Two-layer frontmatter — source vs. computed index
docs.summary: Human-written frontmatter wants to be minimal (ADR-0002). Agents querying the repo want richer structure: reverse edges, resolved references, staleness, summaries, etc. Putting all of that into source…

docs.title: Typed edges with computed reverse edges
docs.summary: ADR tools conventionally have one edge type: `supersedes`. That is not enough to express the relationships a real project has — tasks relate to decisions differently than decisions relate to each othe…

docs.title: STATE.md — auto-generated project snapshot
docs.summary: Agents landing cold in a DocOps repo need a compact, high-signal summary of current state before they start loading individual docs. `.index.json` is machine-optimal; humans and LLMs both also want a …

docs.title: Coverage model — structural gaps and semantic review
docs.summary: Not every ADR will have tasks created for it promptly; not every CTX will translate into an ADR. Without a forcing function, gaps accumulate silently and "implementation: accepted but nothing built" b…

docs.title: ADR `coverage` field — required vs. not-needed
docs.summary: Some ADRs genuinely need no implementation tasks. Examples: - Recording an external constraint ("we are required by law to do X"). - Documenting an already-finished decision retrospectively. - Recordi…

docs.title: ADR `implementation` is computed, not source
docs.summary: The Zund project's existing ADRs carry an `implementation:` field (values: `done | partial | in-progress | not-started`). In practice this field drifts — people forget to update it after merging a tas…

docs.title: CLI with --json over MCP for phase 1
docs.summary: The natural instinct for an "agent-native" tool in 2026 is MCP (Model Context Protocol): structured types, discoverable tools, persistent connection. But for a monorepo where the agent is already runn…

docs.title: Language-agnostic distribution — standalone binary first
docs.summary: DocOps must work in any codebase — Python, Go, Rust, Ruby, mixed, nothing-installed-yet. Requiring the user to install Bun, Node, or any ecosystem-specific runtime creates adoption friction and biases…

docs.title: Ship /docops-* skills for agent tooling
docs.summary: Claude Code, Cursor, and similar agent tools have native concepts for "skills" or "slash commands" — markdown files that package a specific capability into a discoverable slash-command. GStack uses th…

docs.title: Positioning — substrate for LLM-first dev, not harness or personas
docs.summary: The LLM-development tooling space in 2026 is crowded. Adjacent tools (GSD, GStack, BMAD, Spec Kit, Kiro, Agent OS, Backlog.md, Markplane) cover overlapping territory with different philosophies. DocOp…

docs.title: Auto-emit AGENTS.md on init
docs.summary: AGENTS.md has become the de-facto standard in 2025–26 for "agent instructions at repo root" — adopted by Copilot, Codex, Cursor, Jules/Gemini, Factory, Amp, Windsurf, Zed, RooCode, and (via Linux Foun…

docs.title: Meta vs. product layer separation during dog-food
docs.summary: This repository is the DocOps source code AND a DocOps-managed project (dog-food). Both layers want to live in the same repo but must not be conflated. Without an explicit convention:

docs.title: Content search — substring + structured filters; semantic deferred
docs.summary: Agents working in a DocOps repo routinely ask "has this been discussed before?", "what did we decide about rate limiting?", "are there any tasks touching auth?". The current command set (`validate`, `…

docs.title: Read commands are the query API — prefer the CLI over raw `.index.json`
docs.summary: ADR-0005 established the two-layer split: source frontmatter stays minimal; derived fields live in a committed `docs/.index.json` regenerated by `docops index`. The ADR's "consequences" note already h…

docs.title: Ship v0.1.0 from logicwind/DocOps
docs.summary: <!-- AMENDMENTS (body-only until ADR-0025 / TP-026 land the frontmatter schema):   - 2026-04-23 editorial — tap/bucket repo names migrated from     per-tool (homebrew-docops, scoop-docops) to org-wide…

docs.title: First-touch and edit-cycle ergonomics for v0.1.1
docs.summary: First-user testing of v0.1.0 on a real project (`lw-website-nextjs-web`) surfaced three distinct friction points that are not bugs but are design regressions relative to what a polished CLI would do:

docs.title: docops upgrade — in-band refresh of DocOps-owned scaffolding
docs.summary: Between v0.1.0 and v0.1.1 the product added a new `/docops:refresh` skill, removed an obsolete `/docops:next` skill, updated three other skill files with heredoc body examples, and refreshed the `AGEN…

docs.title: skills.sh compatibility — .agents/skills/ as canonical skill store with symlinks
docs.summary: DocOps currently writes skills into two tool-specific directories during `docops init` and `docops upgrade`:

docs.title: Update-check — cached, lazy, opt-out version probe
docs.summary: DocOps ships through Homebrew, Scoop, and direct binary downloads. Once a user runs `docops init`, their project files (skills, schemas, AGENTS.md block) drift from upstream every time we cut a releas…

docs.title: Ship CLAUDE.md alongside AGENTS.md — both share the docops block
docs.summary: DocOps writes its invariants (citation rules, no-edit-STATE.md, schema locations, etc.) into a delimited block in `AGENTS.md` so any coding agent — Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Aider, Copilot, Windsurf…

docs.title: Amendments as first-class decision metadata
docs.summary: ADRs are immutable by convention. This is the right default — decision history must not be silently rewritten. But the convention creates a dead zone for the most common kind of post-acceptance change…

docs.title: ADR implementation delta review — verify shipped code matches decision
docs.summary: DocOps derives ADR `implementation` state from citing tasks (per ADR-0010): once every citing task is `done`, implementation flips to `done`. But "all tasks done" is not the same as "the ADR's Decisio…

docs.title: Static HTML doc viewer for DocOps repositories
docs.summary: Agents and humans browsing a DocOps repository today see markdown files in a terminal or file tree. Raw markdown is functional but lacks the cross-referencing, navigation, and readability of rendered …

docs.title: Multi-harness slash-command delivery via per-runtime translation
docs.summary: TP-023 (commit `379ee51`) moved `/docops:*` from skills to **slash commands** under `.claude/commands/docops/`. ADR-0022 had already solved multi-tool *skills* via a canonical `.agents/skills/` store …

docs.title: DocOps — Vision and North Star
docs.summary: DocOps is a **typed project-state substrate** for LLM-first software development. It lives inside `docs/` in a git repository and gives coding agents three things they need to plan and execute well:

docs.title: Idea evolution — from ADR-only observation to DocOps
docs.summary: Captures the design dialogue that produced DocOps. Non-linear notes, preserved because the pushbacks are themselves design input.

docs.title: Landscape research — LLM-first PM tooling, April 2026
docs.summary: Captured from web research during design. Keeps the "why we are not rebuilding X" reasoning available to future agents.

docs.title: Author constraints and philosophical guardrails
docs.summary: Explicit rules the author (Nachiket) placed on the design. These govern what DocOps will and will not do. Future agents should treat violations of these as red flags.

docs.title: Scaffold the @docops/core CLI project
docs.summary: Create the initial project structure for the DocOps CLI: source tree, build pipeline, cross-compile configuration, release automation. Produce a "hello world" binary that prints a version and exits.

docs.title: Define frontmatter schemas for CTX, ADR, and Task
docs.summary: Implement strict typed schemas matching the final bare-minimum spec in ADR-0002. Output: reusable validators used by every other command and exported as JSON Schema for editor tooling.

docs.title: Implement `docops validate`
docs.summary: Command that validates all DocOps files in a repo against the schemas and the graph invariants. Exit code 0 on pass, non-zero on fail. Designed for pre-commit hooks and CI.

docs.title: Implement `docops index`
Implement `docops state` — generate STATE.md
docs.summary: Command that reads `.index.json` and writes `docs/STATE.md` — the human- and LLM-readable snapshot described in ADR-0007.

docs.title: Implement `docops audit` — structural gap detection
docs.summary: Command that reports structural coverage gaps computed from `.index.json`. Separate from STATE.md: this is the actionable punch list, not a passive snapshot.

docs.title: Implement `docops init` — scaffold DocOps in a repo
docs.summary: Command that turns a bare repository into a DocOps-enabled one. Idempotent — safe to run twice.

docs.title: Implement `docops new <type>` — scaffold documents with ID allocation
docs.summary: Commands that create new CTX, ADR, or Task documents with valid frontmatter, atomic ID allocation, and correct file naming.

docs.title: Publish JSON Schema for editor tooling
docs.summary: Emit the frontmatter schemas as JSON Schema so editors (VS Code YAML plugin, JetBrains, Zed) can validate in-editor without running the CLI.

docs.title: Ship /docops-* skill pack for agent tools
docs.summary: Produce the first version of the DocOps skill pack — markdown files that wrap CLI commands for Claude Code and Cursor so agents get discoverable slash-commands.

docs.title: Implement `docops search` — content + structured filter query
docs.summary: Ship a single command that answers "has this been discussed before?" — substring or regex match over title / tags / body, composable with structured frontmatter filters. Output is the focused slice an…

docs.title: Implement focused read commands — `get`, `list`, `graph`, `next`
docs.summary: Close the query-surface gap identified in ADR-0018 so agents never need to `cat docs/.index.json` to answer routine questions. Each command returns a focused slice of the index, not the whole graph.

docs.title: Finalize skill-pack scope for v0.1.0 — close TP-010 gap
docs.summary: ADR-0019 rescopes the skill-pack work: init-based distribution is the single path for v0.1.0; the `packages/` standalone bundles are deferred. This task closes the remaining gap items from TP-010 that…

docs.title: Release v0.1.0 under logicwind/DocOps
docs.summary: Migrate the module path, add the license, tidy the release pipeline, and cut the first public tag. End state: `github.com/logicwind/DocOps` exists with `v0.1.0` tagged and goreleaser-produced archives…

docs.title: docops init — positional [dir], action announcement, and TTY-interactive confirm
docs.summary: Fix the three ergonomic gaps in `docops init` surfaced by v0.1.0 first-user testing: no way to target a different folder, no explanation of what init will do, no confirmation before writing.

docs.title: docops refresh — composite validate+index+state for the edit loop
docs.summary: Collapse the three-command chain agents and humans run after every doc edit (`validate && index && state`) into a single `docops refresh` subcommand. `audit` stays separate — it is advisory, not a ref…

docs.title: docops new --body flags plus validator enum hints
docs.summary: Let `docops new` create a doc with real content in one CLI call, so agents (and scripts) never have to write-after-create to replace the stub. Also: make validator enum errors self-diagnosing by listi…

docs.title: Implement docops upgrade — targeted scaffold sync
docs.summary: Ship the `docops upgrade` subcommand that pulls the current binary's shipped templates into an already-initialized project without clobbering `docops.yaml` or the pre-commit hook, per ADR-0021.

docs.title: skills.sh-compatible layout — .agents/skills/ canonical + per-agent symlinks
docs.summary: Update `docops init` and `docops upgrade` to adopt the skills.sh canonical layout: skill files live in `.agents/skills/docops/`; tool-specific dirs (e.g. `.claude/skills/docops`) become symlinks. Per …

docs.title: Extract internal/scaffold/ from internal/initter/ (refactor)
docs.summary: Lift the helpers that `docops upgrade` will need to share with `docops init` out of `internal/initter/` and into a new `internal/scaffold/` package, with **zero behavior change**. Sets up TP-018 to la…

docs.title: Implement update-check (cached, gstack-style) + docops update-check subcommand
docs.summary: Ship the update-check mechanism specified by ADR-0023: a small internal package, a standalone `docops update-check` subcommand, and integration into `docops upgrade` so users learn when their binary i…

docs.title: Write CLAUDE.md alongside AGENTS.md in init and upgrade
docs.summary: Implement ADR-0024: docops writes both `CLAUDE.md` and `AGENTS.md` during `init` and refreshes both during `upgrade`. Both files share the same `<!-- docops:start --> … <!-- docops:end -->` block.

docs.title: Restore /docops:* skills for read commands + add orchestration skills
docs.summary: Re-add shipped agent skills for the five CLI commands that landed in TP-011 (`search`) and TP-012 (`get`, `list`, `graph`, `next`) so the bundle satisfies TP-010/013's policy: a skill exists in `templ…

docs.title: Publish docops to npm via per-platform optionalDependencies (esbuild/biome/turbo pattern)
docs.summary: Implement the npm distribution path committed to in ADR-0012's "npm distribution addendum": one thin meta-package depended on by five per-platform packages, where npm's resolver picks exactly the bina…

docs.title: Implement amendments as first-class decision metadata
docs.summary: Ship the machine layer behind ADR-0025: the `amendments:` frontmatter field, the `docops amend` CLI, validator + audit + index + state integration, and the JSON Schema extension.

docs.title: Backfill ADR-0019 tap-rename amendment once the schema lands
docs.summary: Once TP-026 ships the `amendments:` schema and `docops amend` CLI, convert ADR-0019's HTML-comment amendment stub into a proper frontmatter entry using the new machinery. ADR-0019 is the live motivati…

docs.summary: Applied **Option B**: collapsed the dry-run guard and the real-release commands into a single `\`-joined shell block. `exit 0` inside the `DRY_RUN=1` branch now aborts the whole sequence; the `echo > …

docs.title: Implement docops review — reviewed_at ADR field + stale-review detection
docs.summary: Ship the CLI + schema work defined in ADR-0026 so that ADRs whose implementation has flipped to `done` can be review-marked against the shipped code.

docs.title: Implement docops html — static SPA viewer emitter
docs.summary: Implementation lives in `internal/htmlviewer/` (`spa.go`, `bundle.go`, `emit.go`, `index.html`) and `cmd/docops/cmd_html.go`. `docops html [--output PATH] [--base-url URL] [--json]` emits exactly two …

docs.summary: Implementation lives in `internal/htmlviewer/serve.go` + `cmd/docops/cmd_serve.go`. `docops serve [--port N] [--open] [--json]` listens on `127.0.0.1:8484` by default, serves the embedded SPA plus a f…

docs.title: Design + ship default CSS theme for HTML viewer
docs.summary: This task is retained for audit trail and closed without implementation.

docs.title: Ship /docops:* to OpenCode and Codex via per-harness adapter
docs.summary: - Phase 1 (`cf3a2c7`) — `Harness` interface + registry; Claude/Cursor   adapters. Byte-identical output. - Phase 2 (`daa00b6`) — OpenCode adapter + layout-aware writer   (`planHarness`, frontmatter pa…
